sourceRef.citation: Joseba Fernandez de Landa et al. 2026

statement.en: Why are all LLMs Obsessed with Japanese Culture? On the Hidden Cultural and Regional Biases of LLMs
statement.summary: LLMs have been showing limitations when it comes to cultural coverage and competence, and in some cases show regional biases such as amplifying Western and Anglocentric viewpoints. While there have been works analysing the cultural capabilities of LLMs, there has not been specific work on highlighting LLM regional preferences when it comes to cultural-related questions. In this work, we propose a new dataset based on a comprehensive taxonomy of Culture-Related Open Questions (CROQ). The results show that, contrary to previous cultural bias work, LLMs show a clear tendency towards countries such as Japan. Moveover, our results show that when prompting in languages such as English or other high-resource ones, LLMs tend to provide more diverse outputs and show less inclinations towards answer

proposer: Joseba Fernandez de Landa, Carla Perez-Almendros, Jose Camacho-Collados

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The paper presents empirical findings (LLM bias toward Japan in cultural responses) that are reproducible and measurable, but the underlying causal mechanisms remain under investigation. The claim is not yet established as universal law (would require TRUE) nor refuted (would require FALSE), and contradicts some prior work, placing it in provisional/validation phase. The work itself appears to be a preprint (2026 date suggests future/speculative context), characteristic of FLOWING status.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 訓練データの偏りが、文化的な返答分布を予期しない方向へ歪める、謎めいた吸引力。
